request.body: type=config&action=edit&xpath=/config/devices/entry[@name='localhost.localdomain']/vsys/entry[@name='vsys1']/profiles/virus/entry[@name='strict-av-profile']&element=<entry name='strict-av-profile'><decoder><entry name='ftp'><action>reset-both</action><wildfire-action>reset-both</wildfire-action></entry><entry name='http'><action>reset-both</action><wildfire-action>reset-both</wildfire-action></entry><entry name='smtp'><action>reset-both</action><wildfire-action>reset-both</wildfire-action></entry></decoder><description>UIAO-managed strict AV profile — all decoders set to reset-both per KSI-PAN-003</description></entry>&key=<API-KEY>

expected_behavior: Adapter MUST parse response status='success' code=20 and confirm the threat-prevention profile 'strict-av-profile' was updated.
Adapter MUST record that all configured decoders (ftp, http, smtp) have action=reset-both, satisfying KSI-PAN-003 (threat-profile-attached).
Adapter MUST distinguish a profile update (action=edit on a profiles/ xpath) from a rulebase mutation in its audit trail.
Adapter MUST NOT activate the updated profile without a subsequent commit operation.
Adapter MUST capture the profile description field to verify UIAO-managed annotation is present.

primary_citation.quote: Threat prevention profiles are stored under vsys/profiles/virus (antivirus), profiles/vulnerability, and profiles/spyware. The edit command replaces the entire entry at the given xpath.

provenance.sanitization: API key replaced with <API-KEY>. Host replaced with pa-test.example.com (synthetic). Profile name 'strict-av-profile' is synthetic. No real PAN-OS device or production config captured.
